title: Mindful attention to breath regulates emotions via increased amygdala-prefrontal cortex connectivity
headline: Mindful attention to breath regulates emotions via increased amygdala-prefrontal cortex connectivity
summary: A randomized controlled trial of breath-and-attention meditation examined brain & mechanisms. (4) ATB down regulated amygdala activation and increased amygdala-prefrontal integration, with such increased integration being associated with mindfulness ability. Results suggest amygdala-dorsal prefrontal cortex integration as a potential neural pathway of emotion regulation by mindfulness practice.
keyFindings: (2) Left dorso-medial prefrontal cortex was associated with ATB in general.
(4) ATB down regulated amygdala activation and increased amygdala-prefrontal integration, with such increased integration being associated with mindfulness ability.
Results suggest amygdala-dorsal prefrontal cortex integration as a potential neural pathway of emotion regulation by mindfulness practice.
limitations: It is unclear how ATB relevant brain areas in the prefrontal cortex integrate with amygdala activation during emotional stimulation.
Single-trial result; requires replication, and self-reported outcomes may inflate apparent effects.
The practice studied (breath-and-attention meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
